_comment: Trigger eval for deciding skill. 10 should-trigger + 10 should-not-trigger = 20 cases. Negative cases are near-misses: factual lookups, post-decision asks, preference questions without commitment, and other decision-adjacent queries that don't need the six-step framework.

evals.query: /deciding
I'm trying to decide whether to leave my engineering job to go independent. Help me think this through.
should I take the job offer in Berlin or stay in my current role? lots of variables
we're choosing between Postgres, MySQL, and DynamoDB for our event store. need to think through the trade-offs systematically
trying to figure out whether to ship the v2 in January or wait for Q2. tradeoffs are getting fuzzy in my head, can you walk me through it
help me decide between three apartment options. each has different tradeoffs and I keep flip-flopping
I have a real choice to make about whether to take a co-founder on for my project. need to think through it properly, not just go with my gut
should I sell now or hold? this is the kind of thing I usually mess up by not thinking through clearly
trying to choose between two grad school programs. one is more prestigious, the other has better fit. help me work through it
we keep going back and forth on whether to outsource customer support. help me actually decide
what year did Postgres add JSONB support?
I already chose Postgres for the event store. now what's the best schema design?
which is generally better, Postgres or MySQL?
what are the pros and cons of going independent vs staying employed? just curious, not deciding for myself
explain how decision matrices work as a tool
I made a bad decision last month and I'm beating myself up about it. how do I move on?
what's the typical salary range for senior engineers in Berlin?
should I drink coffee or tea this morning?
my partner and I disagree on a big purchase. how do I have that conversation with them?
I keep regretting decisions after I make them. is that normal?
